behavior: Stronger local matching setup streams the complete pinned Qwen3 pair from the pinned anonymous Hugging Face endpoint into app-owned staged files, verifies every locked size and checksum before activation, reports bytes-only monotonic progress, clears the app-scoped transfer and staging caches, and full removal deletes all cached data under the two governed model identities without touching anything else.
revision_scope: The committed governed download, staging, verification, cancellation, and removal implementation at 8bd764c0 plus the opt-in live lifecycle test, fail-closed policy test, and opt-in test policy support added at a2ec0549.

commands.command: node scripts/dev/run-cargo.mjs test -p jobsentinel-local-ai --features embedded-ml governed_download_sets_up_and_removes -- --ignored --test-threads=1
commands.result: The live opt-in lifecycle test downloaded both governed models from https://huggingface.co into a fresh temporary app-data directory, verified readiness, and removed every governed cache.
89 default tests passed with 12 explicit model tests ignored, including the fail-closed anonymous-policy test.

assertions: Both governed caches reached Ready with every locked size and sha256 verified before activation.
Progress reports were monotonic and ended exactly at the locked total of 2406043460 bytes.
The app-scoped Xet transfer cache and per-model staging directories were removed after completion.
Removal deleted both governed model identity roots and reported the runtime as not downloaded.
Without the app-set anonymous policy environment, download fails closed before any network client is built.
caveats: This exercises the production ModelManager path directly on one 64 GiB macOS 27 arm64 host; it is not an installed-app GUI proof, a modest-hardware download measurement, or Windows or Linux evidence.
Network throughput depends on the local connection; the duration is not a release threshold.
Cancellation and resume were verified by focused unit tests, not by interrupting this live transfer.
